hero.subtitle: Modern DICOM imaging for veterinary practice — storage, AI-assisted analysis, and structured reporting built around the Owner → Patient → Study workflow your clinic actually uses. DICOM-native, privacy-first, species-aware.

features.heading: Built for Veterinary Imaging
features.subtitle: Purpose-built imaging and AI tools for companion, equine, and exotic animal practice
features.multiModalDesc: Support for X-ray, CT, MRI, and ultrasound across canine, feline, equine, and exotic patients.
features.realTimeDesc: Get analysis results in seconds with an optimized inference pipeline and GPU acceleration.
features.hipaaDesc: Clinic-grade security with end-to-end encryption, audit logging, and patient data anonymization.
features.clinicalDesc: Models validated against veterinary imaging datasets with transparent, reproducible metrics.
features.precisionDesc: Analysis that accounts for species, breed, and individual patient context.
features.continuousLearningDesc: Models continuously improve while preserving patient and clinic privacy.

useCases.heading: Built for Veterinary Teams
useCases.radiologistsDesc: Automated preliminary reads, anomaly detection, and structured reporting for faster diagnosis.
useCases.emergencyDesc: Rapid triage of urgent findings to support time-critical decisions in the ER.
useCases.researchersDesc: Batch processing, statistical analysis, and model comparison tools for veterinary studies.

testimonials.quote1: The accuracy and speed of VetImage has transformed our radiology workflow. We deliver faster diagnoses without compromising quality.
testimonials.position1: Lead Veterinary Radiologist, Metro Animal Hospital
testimonials.quote2: Having instant AI insights during emergency cases has been a game-changer for our patients' outcomes.
testimonials.position2: Emergency & Critical Care, City Veterinary Center
testimonials.quote3: The research capabilities and model transparency make this platform invaluable for our clinical studies.
testimonials.position3: Director of Veterinary Imaging Research, University Veterinary Hospital
